prerequisites: A MongoDB deployment running as a replica set (MongoDB Atlas or a self-hosted/self-managed replica set). A replica set is required for change data capture (CDC), which this connector relies on.
A MongoDB database user (username and password) that can authenticate against the cluster and read the source databases. SCRAM is MongoDB's default username/password authentication mechanism.
The connection details for the cluster (a MongoDB connection string) and the name(s) of the database(s) containing the collections you want to sync, supplied via the database_config field.
Read access for the connector's user across the cluster. The connector documentation calls for the readAnyDatabase privilege so it can monitor change streams cluster-wide.

To use the Post Image option of update_capture_mode, MongoDB 6.0 or newer with collections configured to return pre- and post-images (changeStreamPreAndPostImages). The default Lookup capture mode has no such requirement.

authMethods.name: Username and password (SCRAM)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a MongoDB database user. Provide the username and password inside database_config (database_config.password is the secret). MongoDB validates these credentials using SCRAM, its default username/password mechanism. For Atlas clusters you can also set the Authentication Source (the database the credentials are validated against, defaulting to admin).

setupSteps.title: Confirm your cluster is a replica set
setupSteps.body: Ensure MongoDB is deployed as a replica set, either MongoDB Atlas (cloud-hosted) or a self-hosted/self-managed replica set. CDC depends on MongoDB change streams, which require a replica set. Note the cluster type, since database_config asks you to choose between an Atlas and a self-hosted configuration.

setupSteps.title: Create a database user and grant read access
setupSteps.body: In MongoDB create a user with a password and grant it read access to the databases you want to sync. To support cluster-wide change-stream monitoring, grant the readAnyDatabase privilege as described in the connector docs. This user's password is the connector's only secret (database_config.password).

setupSteps.title: Gather the connection string and database name(s)
setupSteps.body: Obtain the cluster connection string and the name(s) of the database(s) that contain the source collections. For Atlas clusters, also note the Authentication Source (the database the credentials authenticate against, default admin). You will supply these together with the username and password in the database_config field.

setupSteps.title: Fill in database_config in the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide database_config with the cluster type, connection string, database name(s), username, and password (and Authentication Source for Atlas). This is the only required configuration field; the connector authenticates and discovers collections using these values.

setupSteps.title: Tune the optional sync and CDC settings
setupSteps.body: Optionally adjust the non-required fields: discover_sample_size and discover_timeout_seconds (schema discovery), initial_load_timeout_hours and initial_waiting_seconds (initial snapshot), queue_size (internal buffering), invalid_cdc_cursor_position_behavior (how to react when the saved CDC position is no longer valid in the oplog), and update_capture_mode. Leave update_capture_mode at Lookup unless you enable Post Image, which requires MongoDB 6.0+ and collections configured with pre- and post-images.

sources.title: MongoDB source connector docs (Airbyte): prerequisites, database_config fields, readAnyDatabase privilege, capture mode
MongoDB Authentication: SCRAM is the default username/password mechanism
MongoDB Change Streams (replica set requirement for CDC)
